Title: What gets written to disk
Summary: Saves emit PNG bytes regardless of source format. The .png.import sidecar regenerates on Godot focus.
Body:
## On every save

The editor canvas is encoded as PNG and written to the target path:

```
1. Apply tint (if any) to the working canvas.
2. Apply crop (if any) to produce the final canvas.
3. Encode as PNG base64.
4. POST /api/assets/import with { targetDir, filename, contentBase64, overwrite }.
5. Server validates the path is under the Godot project root.
6. Atomic write: temp file in the same directory, then rename.
```

JPG, WebP, etc. sources convert to PNG. Saves overwrite the source extension if it was something other than `.png` (the source file with the old extension stays only if the new filename was changed; in Edit mode the source file is overwritten in place with PNG bytes under the original name, leaving you with a non PNG named PNG file. This is a known oddity; rename through Duplicate mode if it bothers you).

## Sidecars

The `.png.import` sidecar that Godot writes is not authored by Bleepforge. After a save, the next time Godot focuses the file, it will regenerate the sidecar. Until then, Godot's editor may show a stale preview; reopen the project or hit Refresh in Godot to pull the new pixels.

## Deletes

Right click + Delete in the gallery removes the image AND its `.png.import` sidecar. Without the sidecar removal, Godot would error on next focus. Any `.tres` files holding dangling UIDs after the delete still surface in the [Integrity tab](/diagnostics/integrity); Bleepforge does not auto sweep those.
